envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Dandelion maps the territory. Admins approve the map. Foundation governs what can happen inside the map. DMWs scope what can be remembered. Digital Twins operate within the approved terrain.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Workflow Map intake aligns with ADR-0081 5-stage workflow maturity. Stage 1 (template) + Stage 2 (recommendation) are baseline; Stages 3-5 require admin policy approval + Foundation governance authorization + per-call governance for execution.

items.name: Workflow Map Assessment
items.description: Stage B intake substrate for the Workflow Map. Maps recurring workflows (owners / participants / triggers / inputs / outputs / tools / approval gates / escalation paths). Per ADR-0081, workflows progress through 5 maturity stages; Stages 3-5 require admin + governance approval.
items.human_readable_summary: Asks what recurring processes you run. Outputs Workflow Map candidates pending admin approval. Recommendations are baseline; execution is governance-bound.
items.model_usage_notes: Workflow assessment composes against OOTB workflow templates (docs/ootb-catalog/workflows.json). Templates describe useful defaults; governance authorizes anything that may execute.
required_questions: What recurring workflows does the role / department / company run?
What triggers each workflow (calendar / event / manual / external)?
What inputs and outputs does each workflow consume / produce?
What tools does each workflow require?
optional_questions: What approval gates exist in each workflow?
What escalation paths apply (technical / customer / compliance / legal)?
What safe fallback exists if a connector / tool is unavailable?
What is the workflow's current automation maturity (manual / templated / recommendation / proposed-action / executed)?
Which workflows are recommendation-ready (Stage 2 viable) vs require deeper substrate?
admin_questions: Which workflows should the Map propose at Stage 1 (template-only) first?
Which workflows are eligible for Stage 2 (recommendation-only)?
Which workflows must NOT progress to Stages 3-5 (proposed-action / execution / continuous optimization) without explicit policy review?
Which workflows require dual-control per ADR-0026 regardless of stage?
user_questions: Does the Map represent the workflows you actually run?
Which workflows feel safe to receive recommendations on first?
evidence_sources: Wave 2.1 role-depth files (per-role workflow inventory) at docs/ootb-catalog/role-depth/
output_candidates: Workflow Map proposal (templates / recommendations / proposed-actions / execution candidates)
ADR-0081 stage labels per workflow (Stage 1-5 maturity)
governance_review_points: Admin must approve every workflow stage progression
Workflow Stages 3-5 require Foundation governance authorization + per-call approval per ADR-0081
Dual-control workflows enforced regardless of stage per ADR-0026
Workflow purpose binding required at activation per ADR-0084 §6-layer governance compose stack
Map-region approval per ADR-0082 Amendment 1 remains stronger than entitlement
safe_defaults: Dual-control for risky writes regardless of stage
DMW_scope_implications: Workflow Map bounds workflow-scoped DMW provenance per ADR-0081
Approval-gate workflows constrain Authority Map DMW scope downstream
Escalation paths constrain cross-role DMW visibility
Safe-fallback workflows operate without connector DMW provenance
audit_expectations: future ACTION_* at workflow execution runtime per ADR-0081 Stage 3+
